what_input_feature: An XML input that drives the parser into xmlParseReference (an '&...;' reference) at a point where the current node ctxt->node is NULL, so xmlAddChild receives parent==NULL and the true side at tree.c:3421 fires returning NULL. This requires a well-formed-enough document skeleton (<?xml ...?> + reference token) plus a structural position where no parent node is open.
why_winner_satisfies: The naive winner explores the deep parser call chain (xmlParseReference -> xmlAddChild) and reaches the true side: the per-role overlay shows the winner-only return(NULL) path at 3426-3427, and the divergent-branch table shows the true side hit only in a winner row (L3421 T=18). The winning seed carries a clean '<?xml version="1.0"?>' + '<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "' skeleton that the parser can advance through to reach reference handling.
why_loser_doesnt: naive_ngram4 stays mired in shallow character-data / name-scanning parser states: its L-side hitcounts for low-level scanners (xmlParseCharData, xmlParseName, xmlIsNameChar) are an order of magnitude higher than the winner's, while it almost never reaches the true side of the blocker (L3421 false-side count 194 with true=0). Its seeds are malformed prefixes ('ef="http://...', repeated '"' runs, byte garbage) rather than a clean document head.
mechanism_attribution: The divergence is attributable to the n-gram coverage feedback granularity of naive_ngram4 (the one-technique delta). naive_ngram4 folds a rolling 4-edge history into each map slot, so every distinct 4-edge path through the shallow tokenizers (xmlParseName / xmlParseCharData / xmlIsNameChar) registers as 'new coverage'. This rewards and retains a huge population of near-duplicate shallow-parser inputs, diluting the corpus and energy away from the rare deep path into xmlParseReference -> xmlAddChild. Plain naive's single-edge MaxMapFeedback saturates the shallow tokenizer edges quickly and reallocates energy to the deep reference path, so it reaches the true side. The winner is enabled by the ABSENCE of the ngram granularity.
